mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient corporate output and pricing power, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods yield negative or flat 5-day forward returns in over 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index readings erode real corporate margins and trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL acceleration shows no negative predictive power for 20-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently above 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below 1.0 percent annually signals an imminent economic contraction and reduced corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 70% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below the 1.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening and wage growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal predicts positive 20-day returns in less than 50% of cases over a 4-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a monetary tightening cycle indicate credit market stress and reduced construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines during tightening periods fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with directional accuracy below 48% across all tightening episodes.
